title: Agent Skills Standard and SKILL.md Anatomy · Protocol Clinic: Resource Routing
summary: Create a minimal standards-conforming skill for a repeated review workflow. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext resource routing increment in a valid focused agent skill. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Route the agent to only the references or scripts needed for the current variant.
Write imperative workflow steps, boundaries, inputs, outputs, and verification criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Resource Routing evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-resource-routing-ksk9rj: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Resource Routing into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-resource-routing-ksk9rj:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Resource Routing model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Resource Routing applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Route the agent to only the references or scripts needed for the current variant.
